assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files -- it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only 7 files: review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md, subtask-30001.md, subtask-30002.md, and report.md. All are classification/sub-task/report markdown files. No source code files (.rs, .toml, .sql, etc.) appear in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have corresponding review classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report.md references findings from all comments: comment 30001 (transaction wrapping), comment 30002 (index), and comment 30004 (GET endpoint gap). Comment 30003 (nit) was classified and dismissed. All four were processed.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains no mention of merging, auto-merge, or any merge action. The overall status is 'WARN' with two sub-tasks created and scope concerns noted. There is no indication of any merge operation being performed or triggered.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE -- only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains the row '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test files added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs) |' which exactly matches the expected classification and reasoning.

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) -- the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a detailed '### Convention Upgrade Eligibility Analysis' section with three sub-analyses: (1) CONVENTIONS.md Check (inconclusive -- no file available), (2) Codebase Pattern Analysis (migration directory pattern, SeaORM usage, PostgreSQL soft-delete conventions), (3) Performance-Related Scrutiny (index optimization for high-frequency queries). The upgrade decision explains the convention match: 'The migration pattern in the repository demonstrates an established convention for database schema management' and 'Adding indexes for frequently-queried columns in migrations is a widely established database convention'.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path -- whether classified directly as code change request based on reviewer language, or upgraded from suggestion via convention analysis
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md shows the comment was upgraded from suggestion to code change request via convention analysis: 'The suggestion is upgraded from **suggestion** to **code change request**'. The action states 'Sub-task creation triggered. See subtask-30002.md.' The file subtask-30002.md exists with full sub-task content including Repository, Description, Target PR, Review Context, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria.
